```java
private int getOffsetToAdd(long instant) {
    int offset = this.iZone.getOffset(instant);
    long sum = instant + offset;
    if ((instant ^ sum) < 0 && (instant ^ offset) >= 0) {
        throw new ArithmeticException("Adding time zone offset caused overflow");
    }
    return offset;
}
```

Now, considering whether this mutant is fault adjacent: Fault adjacency refers to whether the change is near a part of the code that was previously buggy or requires special handling. Since the mutation is in the getOffsetToAdd method, which deals with time zone offsets and potential overflows, it's definitely in a critical area where errors can have significant consequences.
